FLR.title: Crude Oil Gains 1%; Fluor Shares Plunge After Q1 Results

macro_events.event_summary: An Iran war has disrupted oil markets to the point that crude has broken decisively above the $100/bbl level, shifting the entire pricing regime higher.
primary_drivers: Sustained crude price spike well above $100/bbl lifting upstream realizations
Middle East supply risk premium and potential Strait of Hormuz disruption rerouting flows
Higher input costs squeezing fuel-consuming industries and consumer discretionary spend

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with ~2.4M bbl/d of upstream production; every $10 sustained move in Brent adds roughly $4-5B to annual operating cash flow. Limited Iran/Hormuz operational exposure relative to peers.
Pure leverage to Brent via Kazakhstan (Tengiz) and Permian; minimal Iranian exposure. Buyback pace accelerates with cash flow at >$100 oil.
Permian pure-play with low breakevens (~$40); incremental dollar of crude flows nearly straight to FCF. No geopolitical operating exposure.
High operating leverage to oil price given debt structure; Permian + onshore weighting means production isn't at risk while realizations soar. Buffett ownership amplifies sentiment bid.
Texas Pacific Land collects royalties on Permian production with near-zero cost base; royalty stream rises 1-for-1 with realized prices in ongoing wells. Pure price leverage.
Cheniere benefits from European/Asian LNG spot premiums spiking alongside oil-indexed LNG contracts; Hormuz risk threatens Qatari LNG flows, redirecting demand to US Gulf cargoes.
Lockheed is the prime contractor on PAC-3, THAAD, and JASSM systems being depleted in active Middle East conflict; supplemental defense appropriations and allied FMS orders accelerate.
Patriot/SM-series missile interceptor production is the direct consumable in Iran-theater air defense; war-driven restocking represents multi-year backlog visibility.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating cost; Brent above $100 with refining crack spreads also widening crushes margins. Limited hedging in 2026 leaves direct passthrough.
Same jet fuel exposure as DAL but with greater international/transpacific route mix that may face airspace closures and rerouting fuel burn penalties around Middle East.
Domestic carrier with historically low fares and price-sensitive leisure demand; jet fuel cost shock combined with weakening consumer at $5+ gasoline pressures both sides of the P&L.
Cruise operator burns marine fuel directly tied to crude; bookings also soften as discretionary travel budgets get absorbed by higher pump prices. Double hit.
Ethylene/petrochemical feedstock costs (naphtha, ethane) rise with crude; commodity chemical pricing power is weak in slowing demand environment, compressing spreads.
Diesel-driven distribution costs and lower-income consumer wallet share both pressured; gasoline price spike is the single biggest historical drag on Walmart traffic and basket size.
Casual dining (Olive Garden, LongHorn) sees direct traffic decline when gasoline crosses pain thresholds; lower-middle income consumer pulls back on discretionary dining first.

Counterintuitive but: prior oil shocks lifted EV demand, however current Tesla story is margin/AI driven, and the broader risk-off, high-rate, weak-consumer regime that follows oil shocks historically compresses TSLA multiple more than it boosts unit demand.

headlines: The Iran war has broken the oil market’s $100 barometer
